rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes; use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Maintain the authoritative source of component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections as the input AADL model
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve the original AADL structure and relationships during fusion
Ensure that all inserted annex blocks are syntactically valid and properly scoped
Do not modify the AADL architecture beyond inserting the generated AGREE annex blocks
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Ensure that all inserted annex blocks are properly scoped and syntactically valid
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not modify the AADL architecture beyond inserting the generated AGREE annex blocks
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
